path: Bus Takayama to Kanazawa and Matsumoto to Takayama.docx
title: Matsumoto to Takayama
snippet: https://www.highwaybus.com/gp/inbound/inbPlanList#reverse Why left side Leaving Matsumoto, the route quickly gets into river valleys and mountain scenery. The better, more consistent “open” views tend to be on the left as you head southwest toward Takayama.

title: 16 full days

title: Hiking
snippet: There is no direct bullet train (Shinkansen) from Hakone to Kyoto. However, you can easily travel between Hakone and Kyoto by using a combination of local trains and the Shinkansen. Here’s how you can make the trip: From Hakone to Odawara: Take the Hakone Toza

title: What to Eat in Kanazawa
snippet: Kanazawa is one of Japan’s best food cities, thanks to its proximity to the Sea of Japan and the bounties of the Noto Peninsula. Authentic, abundant, and deeply traditional, the food here is as good as it gets. Snow crab (kano-gani), oysters, sea urchin (uni),

title: Kanazawa
snippet: Links to blogs and websites with pictures at the end of the doc Wed March 25 Bus Takayama→ Kanazawa (~2h15). Probably arrive at 17:05 or 18:50, drop off our luggage, dinner (sushi at the train station)

title: 1. Start Every Day Before 8 a.m. – During the peak of cherry blossom season in Kyoto, sunrise is around 5:30 a.m. If you are up for it, that’s precisely when your day should begin. Philosopher’s Path, Maruyama Park, the Kamogawa River, and the streets of Gion & the Higashiyama District all never close. Kiyomizudera Temple opens daily at 6 a.m.
snippet: It is not outside the realm of possibilities to hit several of the most popular sakura spots in Kyoto by 8 a.m., before most tourists even leave their hotels. If you think that’s an unreasonably early wake-up call, you’ve probably never experienced the spirit-

title: Day 18-21: Kyoto & Nara
snippet: Kyoto: Explore the city's famous temples and gardens, including Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, and Arashiyama. Hiking: Hike up the Fushimi Inari Shrine (thousands of torii gates) and explore Arashiyama Bamboo Forest and the Hozu River Gorge. Nara: Visit Todai-ji T

title: Matsumoto restaurants
snippet: Chow down on local Japanese cuisine Nagano’s food culture is shaped by the Japanese Alps, and one of the most tantalizing of all the local delicacies is grilled Matsumoto wagyu beef, famous for its sweet aftertaste due to the apple-rich diet of the local cattl

title: Matsumoto
snippet: Sun March 22 Train Shinjuku → Matsumoto (~2.5h). Matsumoto Castle, Nakamachi merchant quarter. https://www.eki-net.com/en/jreast-train-reservation/Top/Index?utm_source=chatgpt.com Bacca brewing for dinner? If not, choose any other place from my doc.

title: Summary of what I read repeatedly on several blogs and discussion threads on tripadvisor, reddit, etc:
snippet: Many people come with carry-ons but pack a bag or buy a bag there for souvenirs to bring back home. Even people who do not normally buy souvenirs say Japanese stuff is cute and regret it if they do not have room for them. BRING A FOLDING BAG for souvenirs. Eve

title: Shirakawa-go

snippet: my two never miss are the sweet potato things from Oimoyasan Koshin and a Melon-pan from Kagetsudo. (opens at 9) Seconding the other commenter on this thread who suggested Kagetsudo for melon-pan bread too. I had mine with chocolate ice cream and it was life-c

title: Central Tokyo: Tokyo station, Marunouchi, Imperial Palace and garden
snippet: Tokyo station is paired well with Akihabara if we did not manage before–a quick ride (4 min) or 25 min walk. Marunouchi | Otemachi Area Guide ︎Tokyo Station (JR Yamanote Line, Tokyo Metro Maruouchi Line), Otemachi Station (Tokyo Metro Tozai Line, Chiyoda Line,

title: Ginza
snippet: Ginza pairs well with central Tokyo (Tokyo station, Maronouchi, Imperial palace etc.) because it is close. It also pairs well with Shinjuku I think. Must-visit spots Ginza Area: Ginza Six Shopping Mall + Rooftop Garden

title: Harajuku
snippet: https://whenin.tokyo/Harajuku-Area-Guide Must-visit spots/experiences: Meiji Jingu Shrine

title: Shibuya
snippet: Shibuya pairs well with Harajuku, Takeshita St, Meiji Jingu shrine. The serene Meiji Jingu shrine is roughly 15 minutes’ walk from the famous crossing in Shibuya. (no subway rides needed). Also good for exploring the neighborhoods of Ometosando and Daikanyama

title: Shinjuku
snippet: Shinjuku is either good with Shibuya or a short ride from central Tokyo (Tokyo station, Maranouchi, Imperial Palace and gardens). Shinjuku is also good – not very far. Best time to visit: late afternoon/ evening Must-visit spots/experiences:

title: On the last two days, we only have Thursday morning set–the national garden. At that moment we can just play with our plan as we wish, skip some things or replace with neighborhoods we did not manage before. It is possible we will not have managed central Tokyo and will do the moat and central Tokyo in the afternoon and shop for souvenirs on Friday. Or if we did not manage Shinjuku or Akihabara, then we will do those.
snippet: Thursday April 2 Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden with Yasuji at 10 am https://www.env.go.jp/garden/shinjukugyoen/news/topics_240301eng.html

title: TOKYO
snippet: This itinerary is just a tentative schedule. We will play it by ear and decide every afternoon whether we are tired and want to change our plans. We will try to keep the neighborhoods that are close to each other or a short ride on a subway. Refer to Tokyo tip

title: Tokyo tips
snippet: Getting from Haneda Airport to Tokyo If you’re flying internationally into Haneda, you’ll most likely arrive at Terminal 3. This airport is closer to central Tokyo, so it’s even easier to get to where you need to go. We recommend you take the Tokyo Monorail. I
